babyDevelopment: The baby is officially a fetus! All major organs and systems are now present in rudimentary form. Fingers and toes are beginning to lose their webbing. Tiny elbows can now flex. The unique tip of the nose is becoming visible.
bodyChanges: Morning sickness often peaks around this time. Your blood volume is rising by 40-50% to support the pregnancy. You might experience vivid dreams due to hormones and disrupted sleep patterns.
tips: Consider genetic screening options with your doctor
Invest in a well-fitting, wire-free bra

do: Keep a small snack like almonds in your bag
Listen to relaxing music to reduce stress
Stay hydrated to support blood volume increase
dont: Worry about 'eating for two' (you only need ~100 extra kcal now)
Perform high-impact sports with fall risks

travelAdvisory.note: Safe to travel; manage nausea proactively.

warningSignsToWatch: Extreme swelling of hands and feet
milestoneDescription: The embryonic stage is over. Your baby is now officially a fetus.
